name: Legacy Community
description_short: Legacy Community Health is a Federally Qualified Health Center (FQHC) in Houston providing comprehensive primary care, preventive services, and pharmacy access to 250,000+ patients across Texas.
description_long:
Legacy Community Health is a community health center serving the Houston area and surrounding Texas regions including Fort Bend, Baytown, Beaumont, and Pasadena. Founded as a Federally Qualified Health Center, the organization provides affordable healthcare access to uninsured, underinsured, and low-income patients through multiple clinic locations. Their mission focuses on removing barriers to care for vulnerable populations through sliding-scale fees and insurance assistance.

Legacy offers a comprehensive range of primary and specialty care services including adult medicine, pediatrics, OB/GYN, behavioral health, dental, vision, pharmacy, endocrinology, HIV/STD screening and treatment, senior care, and school-based health clinics. They accept Medicaid and participate in the Ryan White HIV/AIDS Program. The organization operates three on-site pharmacies with prescription delivery service via text or phone. Virtual visits are available for adult medicine and pediatric services across multiple locations.

Legacy distinguishes itself through community-focused programming such as free Back to School Clinics including developmental screening and food/housing/financial insecurity assessments. They serve 90,000+ pharmacy patients and provide free HIV, syphilis, hepatitis C, and pregnancy testing. The recent partnership with Quest Diagnostics brings on-site lab services with live wait times and dual portal access. Bilingual services in Spanish are available throughout the organization.

As a non-profit FQHC, Legacy provides genuine safety-net healthcare rather than financial products. The organization prioritizes equity in healthcare access and serves populations often excluded from traditional medical systems. However, they are a healthcare provider, not a financial service, and do not offer credit products, loans, or financial counseling related to consumer finance.
services: Pediatric care with virtual visits available
OB/GYN services including free pregnancy testing and prenatal care
Behavioral health and mental health counseling
HIV/STD screening, testing, and treatment including injectable HIV prevention
Senior care and geriatric primary care
pros: Serves 250,000+ patients with comprehensive primary care across multiple Texas locations
Accepts Medicaid and participates in Ryan White HIV/AIDS Program for low-income coverage
Offers three on-site pharmacies with same-day prescription delivery via text service
Provides free HIV, syphilis, hepatitis C, and pregnancy testing without appointment
Free Back to School Clinics with developmental screening and food/housing security assessments
Bilingual services in Spanish throughout all clinics and patient communications
Quest Diagnostics partnership provides on-site lab services with live wait times
cons: Healthcare provider only—cannot help with credit repair, loans, debt, or financial products
Limited geographic service area primarily Houston/Texas region, not nationwide
Walk-in clinics subject to weather closures affecting service availability
best_for: Uninsured and underinsured Texans needing affordable primary care
Medicaid-eligible patients seeking comprehensive health services
Low-income families accessing preventive care and childhood immunizations
diagnosis: Legacy Community Health is a non-profit Federally Qualified Health Center providing affordable healthcare access to vulnerable populations, not a consumer finance service. CreditDoc miscategorized this organization—it should be listed as a free healthcare provider, not in any financial services category. Consumers seeking credit repair, loans, debt relief, or financial counseling should not contact Legacy, as they exclusively provide medical and pharmacy services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
